points.text: Cytosurge AG — CellEDIT platform — Genome engineering — Validated ability to perform multi-guide CRISPR editing in fragile human keratinocytes with controlled dosing, overcoming low transfection efficiency barriers.

Intellia Therapeutics — Lonvoguran Ziclumeran (lonvo-z) — Hereditary Angioedema (HAE) — Phase 3 HAELO trial met primary endpoint with 87% reduction in mean monthly attacks vs placebo (p<0.0001); 62% of patients achieved attack-free and therapy-free status for six months.

Intellia Therapeutics — Lonvoguran Ziclumeran (lonvo-z) — Hereditary Angioedema (HAE) — CRISPR-based gene editing therapy; Phase 3 data published in New England Journal of Medicine showing 91% reduction in moderate/severe attacks and 89% reduction in attacks requiring on-demand treatment.

Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR gene editing platform — Hereditary Angioedema (HAE) — Phase 3 HAELO trial met primary endpoint with 87% reduction in mean monthly attacks (p<0.0001) for lonvoguran ziclumeran (lonvo-z) vs placebo.

Intellia Therapeutics — lonvo-z (NTLA-2002) — HAE — 62% of patients achieved attack-free and therapy-free status for six months vs 11% placebo (p<0.0001); 91% reduction in moderate/severe attacks reported.

Cytosurge AG — CellEDIT — Dermokine knockout in keratinocytes — Enabled deterministic multi-guide CRISPR editing to create isoform-specific knockouts, revealing dermokine's role in cell-cell adhesion and wound healing.

Intellia Therapeutics — Lonvoguran Ziclumeran (lonvo-z) — Hereditary Angioedema — Phase 3 HAELO trial presented at EAACI 2026; primary endpoint met with 87% attack reduction; key secondary endpoints included 62% attack-free rate and 17.04-point improvement in AE-QoL score.

Intellia Therapeutics — lonvo-z (NTLA-2002) — Hereditary Angioedema — Phase 3 HAELO trial presented at EAACI 2026; data published in New England Journal of Medicine showing 89% reduction in attacks requiring on-demand treatment.

Cytosurge AG — Advanced genome editing services — CellEDIT workflow — Deployed to overcome CRISPR delivery challenges in difficult-to-edit models including 3D organotypic skin cultures and fragile primary cells.

Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR gene editing commercialization — Lonvoguran Ziclumeran (lonvo-z) — Phase 3 HAELO trial positive results; 87% attack reduction in HAE; safety profile favorable with mild/moderate TEAEs only.

Intellia Therapeutics — CRISPR-based in vivo gene editing commercialization — lonvo-z (NTLA-2002) — Phase 3 HAELO trial positive results; favorable safety profile with no serious adverse events reported.

Dermokine isoform-specific knockouts — Mechanism: CRISPR-mediated gene editing via CellEDIT — Phase: Preclinical research — Population: Human keratinocytes — Comparator: Traditional bulk delivery methods — Results: Eliminated stress-induced bias, preserved natural phenotype, and confirmed dermokine regulation of keratinocyte differentiation.

Lonvoguran Ziclumeran (lonvo-z) — Mechanism: CRISPR gene editing targeting KNG1 gene to reduce bradykinin production — Phase: 3 — Population: Hereditary Angioedema (HAE) patients — Comparator: Placebo — Endpoints/Results: 87% reduction in mean monthly attacks (p<0.0001); 62% attack-free and therapy-free at 6 months; 91% reduction in moderate/severe attacks.

lonvo-z (NTLA-2002) — Mechanism: CRISPR/Cas9-mediated in vivo gene editing targeting angiopoietin-like 3 (ANGPTL3) — Phase: 3 (HAELO) — Population: Hereditary Angioedema patients — Comparator: Placebo — Results: 87% reduction in mean monthly attacks; 91% reduction in moderate/severe attacks; 17.04 point improvement in AE-QoL score.
